verses.3: Allow me, and I also will speak.
verses.4: As for me, is my complaint to man?
verses.5: Look at me, and be astonished.
verses.6: When I remember, I am troubled.
verses.8: Their child is established with them in their sight,
verses.9: Their houses are safe from fear,
verses.11: They send out their little ones like a flock.
verses.12: They sing to the tambourine and harp,
verses.13: They spend their days in prosperity.
verses.15: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him?
verses.16: Behold, their prosperity is not in their hand.
verses.17: “How often is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out,
verses.18: How often is it that they are as stubble before the wind,
verses.19: You say, ‘God lays up his iniquity for his children.’
verses.20: Let his own eyes see his destruction.
verses.21: For what does he care for his house after him,
verses.23: One dies in his full strength,
verses.24: His pails are full of milk.
verses.25: Another dies in bitterness of soul,
verses.26: They lie down alike in the dust.
verses.28: For you say, ‘Where is the house of the prince?
verses.30: that the evil man is reserved to the day of calamity,
verses.31: Who will declare his way to his face?
verses.32: Yet he will be borne to the grave.
verses.33: The clods of the valley will be sweet to him.
verses.34: So how can you comfort me with nonsense,
